function.name: _table_record_batch_insert
function.description:
Batch insert records into the specified data table.

【Prerequisites - Must Strictly Follow】
Before calling this function, you must complete the following steps:
1. Have obtained the target table's tableId via _query_all_table_list (do not fabricate it yourself)
2. Have obtained the complete field list (fieldList) of the table via _query_table_define, clearly understanding each field's id, name, type, and constraints
3. Based on the prompt requirements and field definitions, construct the complete recordList in this call, with each record containing all required fields with nullable=false
4. Do not call this function when recordList is empty or not constructed
5. Do not add table data unrelated to the prompt

【Field Value Filling Rules】
- Basic types like SingleText / Number / Email / Phone: Directly fill in values of the corresponding type
- User type: Fill in user identifier string
- Select (single choice): Fill in option string value
- Select (multiple choice): Fill in string array, e.g., ["Option A", "Option B"]
- Cascader (single choice): Fill in hierarchical path array, e.g., ["Guangdong Province", "Shenzhen City", "Nanshan District"]
- Cascader (multiple choice): Fill in 2D array, e.g., [["Guangdong Province", "Shenzhen City"], ["Zhejiang Province", "Hangzhou City"]]
- Date / DateTime: Fill in ISO 8601 format string, e.g., "2024-01-01" or "2024-01-01T08:00:00"
- Boolean: Fill in true or false
- UUID / Seq types: Automatically generated by the system, no need to pass in recordList

【Return Value】
Number of successfully inserted records (integer). If 0 is returned, it means no data was inserted; check if recordList is constructed correctly.

tableId.description: Table ID, must come from the id field returned by _query_all_table_list, do not fabricate it yourself.

recordList.description: List of records to insert, must be fully constructed based on field definitions returned by _query_table_define before calling. Do not pass an empty array.

items.description: Single record, keys are field IDs (from fieldList[].id of _query_table_define), values are corresponding field data. UUID and Seq type fields do not need to be passed. Fields with nullable=false must provide values. Example: {"memberName": "Zhang San", "roleName": "Project Manager", "phone": "13800138000", "email": "zhangsan@example.com"}
